mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations embedded in CPIAUCSL force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in UNRATE below 3.5% signals labor market slack that reduces wage inflation pressures, prompting market participants to price in imminent monetary easing and generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in <45% of instances when UNRATE falls below 3.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of supply-driven inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in RSAFS and HOUST indicates weakening consumer and housing demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by >0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in INDPRO during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient productive capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, supporting corporate margins and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS rises by >0.25% monthly, or the mechanism shows negative IC during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between rising CPIAUCSL and stagnant FEDFUNDS creates policy uncertainty and real rate compression, triggering short-term risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS spread shows no significant correlation with 5-day forward returns, or positive returns dominate when the spread widens by >1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS expands the consumer base and boosts aggregate spending, which directly increases S&P 500 earnings expectations and generates positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth is followed by negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations, or the series exhibits zero predictive IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDPC1 exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp demand shock that forces earnings downgrades across cyclical sectors, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in <40% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by >0.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization that outpaces demand destruction, driving short-term momentum and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in >60% of recovery-phase observations, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark during post-shock periods.
